input.background: Refer to Example 5.4 to calculate the standard deviations of the sampling distributions of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ and the sample median $m$. Additionally, determine which statistic appears to be a better estimator of the population mean $\mu$.
input.question: Find the variance and standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $m$.

output.answer:
The variance of the sampling distribution of $m$ is calculated as:
$$
\sigma_m^2=E\left\{[m-E(m)]^2\right\}
$$
where, from Example 5.4, the expected value of $m$ is $E(m)=.460$. Then,
$$
\begin{aligned}
\sigma_m^2 & =E\left\{[m-E(m)]^2\right\}=\Sigma[m-E(m)]^2 p(m) \\
& =(-5-.460)\left(\frac{25}{729}\right)+(0-.460)\left(\frac{612}{729}\right)+(5-.460)\left(\frac{92}{729}\right)=3.801
\end{aligned}
$$
and
$$
\sigma_m=\sqrt{3.801}=1.95
$$

meta_info.caption: Variance of Estimators -Craps Application
meta_info.section: 5.2 Properties of Sampling Distributions: Unbiasedness and Minimum Variance
